title: GST intelligence unearths  ₹1,800 crore tax credit fraud
content:
New Delhi: Directorate General of GST Intelligence (DGGI) has unearthed input tax credit (ITC) fraud of ₹1,825 crore and has arrested an individual behind it, from the New Delhi airport on Sunday, the finance ministry said in a statement on Monday.
The individual, who was running the fraud, was returning from Dubai when DGGI officials held him. The person, the statement said, was wanted in many other economic offences and had not responded to nearly two dozen summons issued by DGGI and had never joined investigations, the statement said.
Investigations showed that a “well-structured arrangement” was used to fraudulently claim ITC and subsequently encash it through refund claims on zero-rated supplies, that is, exports.
Such syndicates use fake invoices to claim tax on raw materials, which are then refunded. Exporters and domestic businesses that cannot fully adjust the taxes paid on raw materials against their output tax liability are issued refunds for the unused tax credits.
Finance ministry said the arrested individual controlled an entire network of dummy firms, employees and close associates.
The entities were created using borrowed ‘know your customer’ (KYC) documents and found to be non-functional or lacking infrastructure, manpower and genuine business activity at the declared premises, the ministry said.
“The dummy proprietors/directors were merely name lenders and were compensated with fixed monthly cash payments. All operational activities including GST registration, invoice generation, banking operations, filing of returns and submission of refund claims were handled centrally by the masterminds,” said the ministry.
Fraud was committed by arranging fake purchase invoices without actual receipt of goods. High-value tobacco products were shown in invoices to create substantial ITC. These invoices were circulated through multiple intermediary firms forming a layered chain of transactions, the statement said.
The ITC so generated was passed from one entity to another through paper transactions, thereby creating an artificial trail of trading activity, it said. This layering enabled the masterminds to introduce ineligible ITC into the GST chain and subsequently accumulate the same in selected entities which were projected as exporters.
Low-value tobacco, inferior smoking mixtures and other tobacco products were procured locally at nominal prices, often without invoices and were subsequently mis-declared as high-value products. Such inflated turnover was used for refund claims, the ministry stated.
Gireesh writes on the Indian economy, government policy, regulatory developments and trends in the business landscape. His areas of reporting include finance, taxation, company law, bankruptcy code, competition law, financial reporting and auditing. He also covers federal policy think tank NITI Aayog. Gireesh has 25 years of experience in leading news organisations.

title: Dubai’s Burj Al Arab hotel shutting down for 18 months for major refurbishment since 1999
content:
Dubai’s Burj Al Arab is heading into an 18-month refurbishment, a move that has drawn attention across the tourism sector. The sail-shaped luxury hotel, one of the city’s most recognisable landmarks, is expected to close during major renovation work. A staff member confirmed the shutdown period, while the owner, Jumeirah, outlined phased upgrades led by Paris-based interior architect Tristan Auer. The project comes at a time when tourism in Dubai appears softer, with travel demand reportedly easing amid wider regional tensions. Some guests with existing bookings will be moved to nearby hotels.
It feels like a long-planned step, yet the scale still stands out. The closure period may shift slightly depending on work progress. Nothing about it feels entirely rushed.
Burj Al Arab set for 18-month closure as major Dubai refurbishment begins
Burj Al Arab is not closing for a short tune-up. It’s a full 18 months. That alone makes it unusual in Dubai’s hotel scene.
People in the industry say large-scale refurbishments like this are rare for such iconic properties. The hotel is aiming for a complete refresh rather than small fixes. The building itself has been a symbol of Dubai’s luxury tourism for years.
That suggests parts of the hotel may be handled in stages, even if operations fully stop for guests. Paris-based interior architect Tristan Auer is leading the renovation. He is known for high-end hospitality design work, often focused on modern luxury styling.
As reported by Reuters, one staff member mentioned that guests with bookings will be supported through alternative arrangements. That part feels quite straightforward. Still, 18 months is a long time for any flagship hotel to step away from full operation.
Burj Al Arab guests relocated as Dubai hotel closure plans take shape
Guests with confirmed bookings are being shifted to nearby hotels in Dubai. That process appears already in motion. Staff communication has focused on continuity of service. Visitors are not being left without options, it seems. There is no suggestion that the hotel brand is stepping back from the market. It is more of a pause, not a withdrawal.
Still, for a property as famous as Burj Al Arab, an 18-month closure feels significant. When it reopens, expectations will likely be higher than before. The building carries that kind of weight in Dubai’s tourism identity.

title: Disturbing video surfaces as Klaudia Zakrzewska hit by Gabrielle Carrington Mercedes after clash outside London nightclub
content:
Klaudia Zakrzewska, known online as Klaudia Glam, is now fighting for her life after a shocking incident outside a London nightclub early Sunday morning. A disturbing video, shared on X, shows a heated fight turning into something far more serious. In the clip, another influencer, Gabrielle Carrington, 29, is seen arguing and physically clashing with Zakrzewska before getting into a black Mercedes. Moments later, the car moves toward Zakrzewska and hits her, leaving people around screaming in panic. One voice in the video clearly shouts, “She’s running her over!” as the situation quickly gets out of control. The car also hits two other people nearby. Reports say the vehicle then reversed over Zakrzewska, who was left lying still on the ground.
Police have not confirmed her condition beyond saying she is in critical state. The incident happened around 4:30 a.m. outside Inca nightclub in Soho, Central London. The area was still busy at that hour, with many people around.
Gabrielle Carrington and Klaudia Zakrzewska clash outside London nightclub turns violent
According to police, Gabrielle Carrington has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, grievous bodily harm, dangerous driving, and drink driving. Authorities believe she was the one driving the car seen in the video.
She is known online as “rielleuk” and has over 360,000 Instagram followers.
Klaudia Zakrzewska, who has around 260,000 followers under the name “klaudiaglam,” had been at the nightclub with friends before the fight began. Reports suggest the argument started inside and later moved outside, where things escalated quickly.
Two other people were also injured during the incident. A man in his 50s, who was unlocking an e-scooter nearby, suffered serious, life-changing injuries and was taken to the hospital. A woman in her 30s had minor injuries and was treated at the scene.
Carrington has a public social media presence where she often shares posts about luxury travel and designer items. In a post from April 7, she is seen standing next to a black Mercedes similar to the one involved in the crash, holding a Chanel handbag. Other posts show her traveling to places like Mexico, Jamaica, and France.
Police are now asking anyone who saw what happened to come forward. Detective Chief Inspector Alixson Foxwell said, “While this incident took place in the early hours of the morning, venues in the area were still open, and we believe a number of people will have seen what happened.”
She added, “I would urge anyone who witnessed the collision, or any activity prior to it that they feel may be of relevance, to come forward.” The investigation is ongoing, and officials are still gathering details from witnesses and video footage.

title: Who is Twin S? Rapper linked in rumours after Klaudia Glam injured in Gabrielle Carrington London crash case
content:
Klaudia Glam’s name is everywhere right now after a shocking incident in central London. Early Sunday morning, the fashion influencer was hit by a car after a street fight involving another influencer, Gabrielle Carrington, also known as Rielle. Police have not confirmed any deaths so far, but they have confirmed a serious crash, injuries, and an arrest. As the news spread, social media quickly filled with videos, reactions, and many unverified claims. One name that suddenly came into the spotlight is Twin S, a UK drill rapper. Many online posts are linking him to the fight, but there is no official proof of that.
Right now, police have not shared any reason behind the incident, and Twin S has not spoken publicly. So, a lot of what people are saying online is still just speculation, not fact.
Klaudia Glam and Gabrielle Carrington London incident and Twin S rumours explained
The incident happened around 4:30 AM on Argyll Street in Westminster, close to a popular club area in London. According to the Metropolitan Police, officers were called after a car hit three people. One woman suffered serious injuries, one man in his 50s had life-changing injuries, and another woman had minor injuries.
Videos online show a heated argument and a physical fight on the street.
People nearby were trying to separate the two women. Moments later, the situation turned worse when a car moved toward the crowd. Witnesses can be heard shouting for the driver to stop.
Police arrested Gabrielle Carrington at the scene. She is facing serious charges, including attempted murder, grievous bodily harm, dangerous driving, and drink driving. The investigation is ongoing, and officials have not confirmed what led to the fight.
Now coming to the name many people are asking about, Twin S. He is a drill rapper from East London, known for his music and growing fan base. He comes from Beckton in Newham and is also part of a duo with his brother, Twin H. His songs like
Feeling Myself and freestyle videos have brought him millions of views. He was also nominated for Best Drill Act at the MOBO Awards and released his mixtape
Black Sheep. He is signed to the label run by rapper Potter Payper.
But here is the important part, there is
no official link between Twin S and the incident. The claims that the fight was about him are only coming from social media posts. Police have not confirmed this, and no reliable source has backed it.
There is also another version going around online. Some people are saying Klaudia Glam may not have been part of the original fight and could have been caught in the middle. Again, this is just a theory and not confirmed by authorities. Right now, the focus remains on the police investigation. Officials are still trying to understand exactly what happened and why.

title: Hi-tech harvester hub scheme: Combine harvesters to be distributed in Mandya
content:
More than 10 combine harvesters will be distributed to farmers in Mandya on Tuesday under the hi-tech harvester hub scheme. The distribution programme will be held on the premises of Mandya University.
The event is being organised by the Department of Agriculture for the year 2025–26 and will be attended by the Minister for Agriculture and the district in-charge Minister N. Cheluvarayaswamy.

title: Election Commission will leave no stone unturned for free, fair and transparent election in West Bengal: CEC
content:
With two days to go for the first phase of the Assembly election in West Bengal, Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar on Monday (April 20, 2026) said elections in the State would be held in a “free and transparent” manner.
“In these elections of West Bengal, the Election Commission of India (ECI) will leave no stone unturned to hold free, fair and transparent elections,” Mr. Kumar said in a statement.
